etymology.description: 匸(匸) (xì, "concealed") + 品(品) (pǐn, "goods") = many people or goods concealed in a space -> area
etymology.interpretation: The character 區 (qū, "area") illustrates the concept of an enclosed space with multiple items or individuals represented by 品(品) (pǐn, "goods") within 匸(匸) (xì, "concealed"). This evokes the sense of a defined area or district where such items are contained.
etymology.simplification: The traditional component 品(品) (pǐn, "goods") was replaced by 乂(乂) (yì, "to govern") in the simplified form, 区 (区), keeping the enclosure concept.

examples.chinese: 这一区的房价很高(這一區的房價很高)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè yī qū de fángjià hěn gāo.
examples.english: The house prices in this area are very high.

examples.chinese: 他们住在城市的南区(他們住在城市的南區)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen zhù zài chéngshì de nán qū.
examples.english: They live in the southern district of the city.

examples.chinese: 这个区以其丰富的文化遗产而闻名(這個區以其豐富的文化遺產而聞名)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège qū yǐ qí fēngfù de wénhuà yíchǎn ér wénmíng.
examples.english: This area is famous for its rich cultural heritage.
